subject: Social Science (History)
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the political and social challenges faced by India after independence
Analyze the importance of the Indian Constitution in establishing democracy
Explain the process and reasons behind the reorganization of states
Understand India's economic development strategies such as the Five-Year Plans
Recognize the role of major leaders in shaping modern India
Analyze how diversity and unity were maintained in independent India
Evaluate the progress and continuing challenges of the nation after independence

lesson_plan.title: India in 1947: Challenges of a New Nation
student_activities: Prepare a short timeline of events after independence
Discuss the challenges faced by the new government

lesson_plan.title: Framing of the Indian Constitution
topics_covered: Role of Dr. B. R. Ambedkar
student_activities: List important features of the Constitution
Discuss why a constitution is necessary for a nation

lesson_plan.title: Reorganization of States
student_activities: Mark states formed after reorganization on a map

lesson_plan.title: Economic Planning and Development
student_activities: Prepare a simple chart of Five-Year Plan objectives
Discuss why planning was important for development

lesson_plan.title: Social Development in Independent India
student_activities: Discuss examples of social reforms after independence
Write a short paragraph about the importance of education

lesson_plan.title: Unity and Diversity in Modern India
student_activities: Discuss how unity can be maintained in diversity

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze how different political and economic decisions shaped modern India. Using timelines and maps can help students visualize the development of the nation after independence.
